title: Bindings, Scope, Closures, and Lifetime · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: Build configurable counters and validators without leaking mutable state across community-service widgets. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a closure-backed state and validation library. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose const, let, or legacy var from reassignment, scope, and initialization behavior.
Resolve names through lexical environments and identify shadowing without guessing from call order.
Predict declaration instantiation, function hoisting, and temporal dead-zone failures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.javascript:
// javascript-basics cumulative stakeholder program.
// Add one runnable behavior, assertion, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Let Const Var · changed case
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Create two independent status counters seeded at 5 and 8. Calls must preserve closure isolation, explicit inputs and returns, and a trace showing why a shadowed binding does not update outer state. Competency probe: Choose const, let, or legacy var from reassignment, scope, and initialization behavior.

Which action demonstrates Let Const Var without dropping earlier contracts?
